claim:
The Amazon spans more than 5.5 million square kilometers, primarily in Brazil which holds
about 60% of the forest.
label: SUPPORTED
top_evidence:
The Amazon rainforest covers over 5.5 million square kilometers across nine countries,
with Brazil containing approximately 60% of the forest.

claim: It contains roughly 10% of Earth's species.
label: SUPPORTED
top_evidence:
It is home to an estimated 10%
of all species on Earth.

claim:
Deforestation has
accelerated, with 15,000 square kilometers cleared in 2020.
label: UNVERIFIABLE
top_evidence:
Scientists estimate that over 11,000 square kilometers were
cleared in 2020.

claim:
The region is home to around
2 million indigenous people from over 400 ethnic groups.
label: CONTRADICTED
top_evidence:
The indigenous population numbers approximately 1 million people
from around 400 distinct ethnic groups.

claim:
The Amazon River contributes
20% of global freshwater ocean discharge.
label: SUPPORTED
top_evidence:
The Amazon River releases about 20% of all
fresh water that flows into the world's oceans.
